county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Macon. County seat: Kirksville.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Kirksville holds land records from 1841. Adair County was named for John Adair, Kentucky governor. Truman State University (formerly Northeast Missouri State) in Kirksville has local history collections in Pickler Memorial Library. The Adair County Historical Society maintains family files and cemetery transcriptions. Mormon settlers passed through during the 1838 Missouri-Mormon War — check LDS Church History Library for related records. Northeast Missouri is predominantly Scots-Irish and English in settlement heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives in Jefferson City holds circuit court naturalization records for Adair County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

source_profiles.source_name: Adair County Deed Records -- Recorder of Deeds, Kirksville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1841 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation (1841)

source_profiles.source_name: Adair County Probate Records -- Probate Court, Kirksville
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estate inventories, administrations, and guardianship records from 1841. Missouri probate courts also handle some adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Adair County Marriage Records -- Recorder of Deeds / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records from 1841. Missouri marriage records kept at county level by Recorder of Deeds. FamilySearch has indexed many Missouri marriages.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1841-1950 for Adair. Missouri territorial census 1820; state census 1844, 1852, 1856, 1860, 1864, 1868, 1876 (incomplete). Slave schedules 1850, 1860.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Generally well preserved at Missouri State Archives

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Platte Purchase. County seat: Savannah. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Savannah holds land records from 1841. Andrew County was part of the Platte Purchase (1836), land acquired from Native American tribes — pre-1836 records are in federal Indian agency files at NARA-KC. The Andrew County Historical Society in Savannah has genealogical files. Savannah was an important steamboat landing on the Missouri River. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky, Tennessee, and Virginia. Some enslaved persons records in probate court estate inventories pre-1865. FamilySearch has microfilmed Andrew County records. Missouri State Archives holds early land entry records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1845 from Holt. County seat: Rock Port. Part of the Platte Purchase (1836) — last major land acquisition from Native Americans in Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Rock Port holds land records from 1845. Northwestern Missouri county on the Missouri River. Atchison County Historical Society in Rock Port maintains local genealogical files. Strong Midwestern farming community — German and Scandinavian settlers. BLM General Land Office patents document original land entries. Atchison County Museum has pioneer-era artifacts and family records. FamilySearch microfilm includes deed books and marriage records. Check Holt County for pre-1845 records since Atchison was carved from it.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1836 from Ralls. County seat: Mexico. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Mexico holds land records from 1836. Audrain County Historical Society maintains extensive genealogical files including cemetery transcriptions. Mexico was a Civil War flashpoint — skirmishes occurred but courthouse survived. The county attracted Southern-heritage settlers from Virginia and Kentucky. Audrain County has excellent saddle horse breeding history — check agricultural society records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books, probate, and marriage records from the 1830s forward.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1836

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1835 from Greene. County seat: Cassville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Cassville holds records restarting after 1872 courthouse fire that destroyed most early records. For pre-1872 research, check Missouri State Archives for surviving fragments, BLM GLO records for original land patents, and neighboring county records. Barry County Heritage Center in Cassville has post-fire genealogical collections. Roaring River State Park area was Osage and Delaware territory — check NARA-KC for Indian agency records. Strong Scots-Irish and Southern Appalachian settlement heritage. FamilySearch microfilm begins from the post-fire restart period.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1835

county_metadata.record_losses: Civil War destruction; courthouse burned 1860; records largely destroyed by guerrilla warfare
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1855 from Jasper. County seat: Lamar. Burned county: Civil War destruction; courthouse burned 1860; records largely destroyed by guerrilla warfare
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Lamar holds records restarting after Civil War destruction. The 1860 courthouse fire and subsequent guerrilla warfare destroyed most early records. Barton County was severely affected by Bushwhacker-Jayhawker violence along the Kansas-Missouri border. For pre-war records, check Missouri State Archives, BLM GLO records, and neighboring Jasper County. Harry S Truman was born in Lamar in 1884 — Truman Birthplace State Historic Site. Barton County Historical Society maintains post-war genealogical files. Lead and zinc mining district records supplement county records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1855

county_metadata.record_losses: Order No. 11 (1863) forced depopulation; courthouse burned; most pre-1863 records destroyed
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Cass. County seat: Butler. Affected by Order No. 11 (1863) — forced depopulation of western Missouri border counties during Civil War. Burned county: Order No. 11 (1863) forced depopulation; courthouse burned; most pre-1863 records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Butler holds records restarting after 1863. Order No. 11 (August 1863) forced the depopulation of Bates County — virtually all civilians expelled and structures burned. This is one of Missouri's most severely affected burned counties. For pre-1863 records, check Missouri State Archives for any surviving copies, BLM GLO land patents, and early census records (1850, 1860). Bates County Historical Society in Butler has post-war resettlement records. Many families fled to other counties or Kansas during the war. FamilySearch microfilm covers the post-war restart. Strong Southern heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1835 from Pettis, Greene. County seat: Warsaw.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Warsaw holds records restarting after 1848 courthouse fire. For pre-1848 research, check Missouri State Archives for any surviving fragments and BLM GLO records. Benton County is situated on the Osage River and Lake of the Ozarks. Benton County Historical Society in Warsaw has family files. Strong Southern settlement heritage from Kentucky and Tennessee. Significant Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse had already burned before the war. Harry S Truman practiced as county judge in this area. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-fire deed books and probate records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1851 from Cape Girardeau, Madison, Stoddard, Wayne. County seat: Marble Hill.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Marble Hill holds land records from 1851. Bollinger County was named for George Frederick Bollinger, an early German settler. Strong German heritage — check German Reformed and Lutheran church records. Bollinger County Museum of Natural History. The county has significant Whitewater River valley settlement history. Cape Girardeau County holds pre-1851 records for this area. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Some French colonial-era records relate to this area through Cape Girardeau district.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Howard. County seat: Columbia. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Columbia holds land records from 1820. Boone County is home to the University of Missouri (Mizzou, founded 1839) and the State Historical Society of Missouri (SHSMO), which is the premier repository for Missouri genealogical research — holding newspapers, manuscripts, county histories, and family papers statewide. SHSMO's newspaper collection is extraordinary and fully indexed. Columbia Public Library has a genealogy section. Boone County Historical Society and Boone County Heritage Museum preserve local records. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement — Daniel Boone's sons settled here. Significant enslaved persons records in probate court. Boone County is one of Missouri's most well-documented counties for genealogical research.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1820
